summary: Frontend smoke + targeted regression test for the two bug fixes in iteration 2: (a) Hero 4th chat bubble now reads 'Accessing your camera…' (no longer 'Looking at your screen now'), and (b) Emotions calm tab renders the quote with real apostrophes — no ''' literal anywhere on the rendered page. Backend skipped per request (was 100% green in iteration_1).

tests_executed.check: Hero chat bubbles contain 'Accessing your camera…'
Hero chat bubbles do NOT contain 'Looking at your screen'
Clicking emotion-tab-calm shows 'Take your time...' quote
Calm quote contains real apostrophe (ASCII ' or U+2019)
tests_executed.result: PASS — rendered as straight ASCII apostrophes: "Take your time. I'm here whenever you're ready."

tests_executed.check: Calm quote does NOT contain literal '''
Hero waitlist submit shows position result

tests_executed.result: PASS — shows 'Hold on — let me look at that more carefully.'

NOT VERIFIED via testid — selector [data-testid^="faq-item-"] did not match. FAQ section is visible in screenshot and appears to render correctly, but its data-testid attributes (if any) were not exercised. Not a regression of this fix; informational only.
PASS — input/button found and clicked without error

action_items: Optional: add data-testid attributes to FAQ accordion items (e.g., data-testid="faq-item-{n}" or "faq-toggle-{n}") to make accordion behaviour testable in future iterations. Current iteration's fixes are not affected.
critical_code_review_comments: Both fixes are clean and minimal: Hero.jsx ~line 239–241 uses 'Accessing your camera…' inside ChatBubble (role=lucy, typing). Emotions.jsx line 58 uses real ASCII apostrophes in calm.line, and line 167 renders {active.line} directly inside curly quotes (“{active.line}”) with no .replace() — exactly as specified.

Note: other strings on the page still use JSX entity ' in source (e.g., Hero h1 'SHE'S NOT', Emotions h2 'doesn't answer', Hero first chat bubble 'I'm Lucy') — these all render as real apostrophes in the DOM (verified via document.body.innerText scan).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (9/9 critical checks; FAQ testid absence is informational only)

seed_data_creation: Submitted test waitlist emails: test_smoke@neural.link (hero), test_footer@neural.link (footer). Backend was not retested; these entries may live in the waitlist collection.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 2 verified two cosmetic bug fixes only. Hero 4th chat bubble = 'Accessing your camera…'. Emotions calm tab quote = "Take your time. I'm here whenever you're ready." with real apostrophes. No ''' literal anywhere in rendered DOM. Smoke checks on waitlist (hero + footer), emotion tab switching (calm + focus) all passed. FAQ accordion items currently have no [data-testid^="faq-item-"] selector — if a future test needs to assert FAQ behaviour, ask main agent to add data-testid to FaqItem buttons.
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug: visible literal 'you're' was rendered in the Emotions 'LUCY SAYS' panel because the source string used ' inside a regular JavaScript string literal (not JSX text) — JSX entity decoding only applies to text nodes inside JSX, not to string values stored in a JS object's property. Fix correctly switches calm.line to use real ASCII apostrophes so the raw string value matches what is rendered. Hero typing bubble copy change is a simple string update. Both verified against the production preview URL.
